links.rationale: BOTH surfaces now realized. REST (F-011): @tessera/api route Zod schemas (zod/v4, fastify-type-provider-zod) are the single source for validation + serialization + OpenAPI (GET /v1/openapi.json). MCP (F-012): @tessera/mcp tools wrap the SAME domain services (type-only ApiServices import, so no Fastify in the MCP runtime) with the same masked error-envelope policy. Changing a shared domain service, the error-envelope shape, or a route/tool contract must be reflected across REST + MCP + the regenerated OpenAPI + the SDK + the dashboard — additive/versioned (NFR-11). Both surfaces take injected services; deployment-profile wiring + the bootable processes are F-015. F-047 added the DSR + retention surface. Its response schemas REUSE memorySchema/graphNode/graphEdge/sourceSchema/auditEventSchema, so any change to those ripples into the DSR bundle contract too (that coupling is deliberate — the export must mirror what the surface serves). KnowledgeGraphService gained exportAll/purge (service-level only, over existing GraphStore methods — no port change), which the DSR bundle depends on being UNBOUNDED, unlike the display-capped queryGraph. F-048 made the contract observable end-to-end: the suite pins the real shapes rather than test doubles — search hits are `{ref: <sha256>, score, signals}` with NO label (F-073), compile fragments carry `text` + provenance, file graph nodes are keyed source-relative + extensionless (`src/ledger`). Those are contract facts the stubbed suites never asserted; changing them breaks e2e-full first. F-081 CHANGED THIS CONTRACT, and it is the first breaking response change on /v1: POST /v1/sources/:id/scan went 200 {source, summary} -> 202 {source, state, progress?}. The summary CANNOT survive that — a request that no longer waits for the ingest has nothing truthful to say about what changed; it now arrives via GET /v1/sources/:id/scan (lastScan) or the source.scan.completed event. Dependents resolved IN THE SAME CHANGE, and TypeScript named every one: the OpenAPI doc (regenerated — note generate.mjs imports the BUILT @tessera/api, so a stale dist silently regenerates the OLD spec and the type error lands in the SDK instead), the SDK (ScanResult -> ScanAccepted, keyed off responses[202]), and the dashboard (which read result.summary to toast 'Scan complete' — now a lie, so it says 'Scan started'). THE MCP SURFACE IS DELIBERATELY UNCHANGED: scan_source still calls the synchronous SourceService.scan() and still returns the summary, because an agent asking 'scan and tell me what changed' wants the answer, not a job id. That is the one place ADR-0036 REST/MCP parity is intentionally asymmetric — the surfaces have different callers with different needs, and the shared engine (one performScan) is what parity actually protects. The SSE contract also grew: source.scan.progress/.failed joined started/completed, and started gained a `total` field so a client can draw a determinate bar from the first frame. F-084 added GET /v1/stats/activity (the Overview activity chart): OpenAPI + SDK (getActivity) + dashboard, resolved in the same change. Deliberately DASHBOARD-ONLY, no MCP tool (ADR-0053): an agent has no use for a histogram, and get_stats stays its summary. /v1/stats itself is UNCHANGED — its documented refusal to carry trend fields stands. Same SDK-regen trap as F-081: generate.mjs imports the built @tessera/api, so rebuild it first or the old spec regenerates. F-088: /v1/stats/activity gained tzOffset (minutes east of UTC, validated -720..840) so buckets are the VIEWER'S calendar days — additive, default 0 preserves F-084 UTC behavior exactly; OpenAPI + SDK regenerated in-change (same rebuild-first trap). F-089 added GET /v1/stats/activity/recent (stats:read): the last N successful work actions, a deliberately NARROWED member-visible view of the audit trail (success only, work actions minus search, non-sensitive fields only — no outcome/metadata; NFR-7). Not audited (the /v1/stats posture), no MCP tool (ADR-0053). The dashboard feed + bell now render THIS instead of an in-memory session store; OpenAPI + SDK (getRecentActivity) regenerated in-change. F-089 added GET /v1/stats/activity/recent (stats:read): the last N successful work actions, minus search — a deliberately NARROWED member-visible view of the audit trail (success only, non-sensitive fields only), so the Overview feed + bell read persisted data; the full trail stays admin-only at /v1/audit. Not audited (the /v1/stats posture), no MCP tool. OpenAPI + SDK (getRecentActivity) regenerated in-change.

Tokens-only, verified by screenshot across 4 themes x light/dark. TWO REAL BUGS CAUGHT BY SCREENSHOT/AXE THAT NO UNIT TEST WOULD HAVE: (1) the Card base sets `dark:ring-0`, which BEATS an unprefixed `ring-2` — the active-row cue was invisible in all four dark themes while correct in light; `dark:ring-2` is load-bearing, not redundant. (2) `aria-controls` pointed at the results listbox, which does not exist until there are results — a CRITICAL dangling-ARIA violation on the empty search page, found by the auth e2e (which lands on /search with no query) and now pinned by an empty-state axe test. Same rule as aria-activedescendant: an ARIA reference must point at a live element.

Inspector v2 (F-062): the empty-package GUIDANCE state, agent-ready export, kind filters + budget presets, the clamp notice, and a session recent-compiles list — tokens-only across 4 themes x light/dark, screenshot-verified. THE FIX IS A RENDER RULE, NOT ARITHMETIC: computePackageScores([], budget, 0) returns budgetAdherence 1 / provenanceCoverage 1 / redundancy 0, and every one is a defensible VACUOUS TRUTH (the package did not exceed budget; 100% of zero fragments carry provenance). The lie was rendering that as three full bg-primary bars with aria-valuenow=100. So the scores are gated on fragmentCount > 0 in apps/web and the compiler is untouched — making PackageScores nullable would ripple through quality.ts/explain/the schemas/the SDK for zero user gain. Tests assert on role='progressbar' having COUNT 0, not on the '100%' string: aria-valuenow was half the lie, announced to screen readers. Also: fragment citations are now provenance.source.path (F-073's disease survived in the Inspector after /search was cured — cure needed no contract change, the compiler already forwards corpus metadata).

components/ui/data-table.tsx (F-063) — the virtualized data-table pattern. A virtualized table CANNOT be a <table>: absolutely-positioned rows leave the table row-box algorithm (columns stop aligning) and `display:block` strips the implicit ARIA roles — so the grid roles are DECLARED. Two deliberate structural collapses: the role=table element IS the scroll container (a div between it and the rowgroups breaks aria-required-children, and it is also what makes `sticky top-0` work — the clean chain and the sticky header are ONE structure), and the virtualizer's height spacer IS the body rowgroup (rows become direct children; no role=presentation gymnastics). `aria-rowcount={-1}` is load-bearing: cursor pagination means the total is genuinely unknown (AuditPage is {events, nextCursor?} — no count exists anywhere), and announcing the LOADED count would say 'row 50 of 50' while nextCursor proves otherwise — the 'narrow the filters' lie whispered to the users least able to detect it. `aria-rowindex` is ABSOLUTE (+2 for the 1-based header), which axe CANNOT check — a window-relative index is structurally valid ARIA and totally broken for AT, so it gets an explicit RTL assertion at a scrolled window. components/ui/table.tsx stays for small STATIC tables (native semantics, no ARIA to get wrong) and its three consumers are NOT migrated. NO @tanstack/react-table: it is headless (no DOM, no ARIA — the hard part is ours either way) and every row model it provides (sort/filter/paginate) is bypassed by the server; what remained would be a dependency for `.map()`.

The manifest is the agent-facing projection of the same contract and must stay in sync.

Mirrored by the "Full-stack E2E (gate 9)" CI step (verify-state enforces the mirror). Added a `tests/*` glob to pnpm-workspace.yaml — the workspace is no longer just apps/* + packages/*.

tests/bench (@tessera/bench) — the `perf` gate (F-049; NFR-4), ACTIVE: `pnpm -w bench`. tests/web-perf (@tessera/web-perf) — the `web-perf` gate (ADR-0021), ACTIVE: `pnpm -w test:perf`. Both mirrored by CI steps (verify-state enforces it). web-perf gates the BUNDLE budget only; CWV is F-074 — the gate description states that scope limit rather than implying NFR-17 is covered. web-perf runs ONLY @tessera/web-perf (`turbo run test:perf --filter=@tessera/web-perf`): unfiltered, turbo gave EVERY package a no-op test:perf node whose `^build` rebuilt the whole monorepo while building NEITHER app the gate measures. It therefore REQUIRES gate 5 (build) to have run first — the ORDERED gate list is that contract — and now fails fast saying so instead of waiting 120s on a server that will never start. The gate owns real server processes: it spawns `next start` DIRECTLY (never via `pnpm --filter`/a shell) and kills the process TREE, because child.kill() reaches only the direct child; the orphaned server kept our stdio pipes open, the event loop never drained, and the gate hung forever after printing PASS.

The `perf` gate DID its job on F-061: it failed at 918/900 when label+kind+node all rode by default, and thresholds.json was NOT touched (per its own line 3). Measuring per-field disproved the plan’s arithmetic — `node` cost 135 and RESTATED the label (node.key is the path minus the extension) — which produced the opt-in design. Re-measured at 783/900 (117 spare, 13%). Search p95 rose ~60% (5.6ms -> ~8.9ms) from the per-hit corpus lookup, still ~34x under the 300ms NFR-4 ceiling. The gate boots two real servers + a browser + an MCP process on ports 3200/3201, chosen so it can run back-to-back with gate 6 (3000/3100) in one CI job; CI must install the Playwright browser for @tessera/e2e-full too (it pins the same @playwright/test, so it is one shared browser build). Both were designed against CI hardware variance: perf asserts ABSOLUTE NFR-4 thresholds (never baseline deltas) with fake embeddings so the engine sits orders of magnitude under the ceiling, and web-perf asserts a byte count, which carries no timing variance at all. 2026-07-17: the `security` job no longer runs `pnpm audit` — npm retired the legacy audit endpoints on 2026-07-15 and the fix exists only in pnpm 11, so the job had been erroring (failing OPEN) over a real critical + high. `audit.export` added to AUDIT_ACTIONS (F-063) — FR-55 names 'exports' as an audited category in its own text. DISTINCT from `audit.read` on purpose: a client looping /v1/audit to completeness would record N audit.read events, INDISTINGUISHABLE from an admin scrolling, so 'who took a copy of the trail?' would be unanswerable. The recorder reads the action from STATIC route config (recorder.ts), so it cannot vary per request — which is why an `?export=true` flag on /v1/audit was structurally impossible and a separate route was the smaller change. Adding an action does NOT break the MCP taxonomy: MCP_AUDIT_ACTIONS is Record<McpToolName, AuditAction>, exhaustive over TOOL NAMES and valued by action, so a new action is a new value not a new key. The web's AUDIT_ACTION_LABELS is an exhaustive Record<AuditAction,string>, so typecheck DEMANDED the label and the action-filter dropdown picked it up for free. NOTE (SL-4): AuditEvent.metadata is a write-dead field — nothing in the product populates it — so the export event records WHO/WHEN/that-it-was-an-export, but not WHICH FILTERS. An audit route importing from dsr/ would have been the wrong dependency direction. FIXED WHILE HERE: sqlite-audit-log had `query.limit ?? 50` with NO clamp while the in-memory adapter clamped to MAX_AUDIT_PAGE_SIZE — a port contract only the REFERENCE adapter honoured. Unreachable over HTTP (the route schema caps limit) but the export walks the PORT directly, below that schema, which is exactly where it lived.

links.rationale: F-027 audit trail (FR-55/NFR-13): sensitive actions are recorded at the /v1 boundary into an append-only, tenant-scoped AuditLog and queried by admins (admin:manage). Changing the AuditEvent model, the AuditLog port, the recording hook's action catalog (config.audit), or the /v1/audit route/schema ripples to the adapters + shared conformance, the OpenAPI/regenerated SDK (E-003), the config wiring (E-014), and the governance UI (FR-48). Events are NON-SENSITIVE (NFR-7 — ids/actions/outcomes only); recording is failure-isolated; tenancy reuses forTenant (ADR-0033) with the actor/tenant from the AuthContext (E-018). Default is an in-memory sink so existing surfaces are unaffected. Increment 1a = in-memory end-to-end; 1b = persistent SQLite + config wiring; increment 2 = the web UI. F-047 closes F-027's MCP seam: the trail now covers BOTH surfaces on ONE taxonomy, so a compliance query never has to union two vocabularies (ADR-0036 parity). Two invariants ride on this: (1) a new MCP tool MUST be added to MCP_AUDIT_ACTIONS (the Record<McpToolName,AuditAction> makes omission a typecheck error, and a unit test pins the key set against TOOL_PERMISSIONS); (2) a new AuditAction must be mirrored in apps/web (types + labels) or web typecheck fails. Recording is best-effort/failure-isolated on both surfaces — a sink error must never fail a request or a tool call. F-084 grew the AuditLog port with activity(query) -> { buckets, earliest }: per-UTC-day GROUP BY of "work" actions (ACTIVITY_ACTIONS = AUDIT_ACTIONS minus *.read), plus MIN(at) over the whole tenant trail as the retention floor (ADR-0053 clause 3 — so a chart never draws a pruned day as silence). Both adapters move together: the shared conformance covers the in-memory reference; the SQLite adapter gets focused tests in its own file (the F-063 precedent, pointer to F-078) since it still does not run the shared suite across the package boundary. The SQL is real (substr(at,1,10) group + min) and proven by reverting the inArray filter. computeWorkspaceActivity (Fastify-free, like computeWorkspaceStats) does the honest windowing + zero-fill above the store. F-088 grew ActivityQuery with tzOffsetMinutes: the day bucketing shifts by the viewer's offset INSIDE the store aggregation (SQLite date(at, n-minutes) modifier / in-memory shifted toISOString) and the floor clamp compares the local day of earliest — both adapters moved together with conformance + focused SQLite tests, per this link's standing rule. F-089 grew AuditQuery with actions (a multi-action IN filter): in-memory matches() + SQLite inArray, pinned by the shared conformance + a focused SQLite SQL test, per this link's standing rule. The recent-activity endpoint (E-003) is its first consumer. F-089 grew AuditQuery with actions (multi-action IN filter) for the recent-activity feed — both adapters + shared conformance + focused SQLite tests moved together, per this link's standing rule.

links.rationale: F-044 adds a cross-cutting hardening layer at the /v1 boundary: security headers (explicit hooks, no @fastify/helmet — the JSON API needs a strict CSP), a RateLimiter port + in-memory fixed-window adapter mirroring the F-026 MCP QuotaLimiter (a distributed/persistent limiter is the documented seam), a per-profile CORS allowlist (ADR-0035 app↔api cross-origin), and request-id correlation threaded into logs (Fastify reqId) + traces (observability span attribute). All additive and safe-by-default (headers on; rate limiting off; loopback CORS; no HSTS) so existing routes/tools/e2e are unchanged (advances E-003). Extending the header set, swapping in a distributed rate-limiter, adding API-key principals (F-055), or changing the request-id contract ripples through these modules + their config wiring (E-014) + the observability seam (E-015) + the auth keying (E-018).

links.rationale: F-045 makes the dashboard a real auth client while keeping zero-auth Local mode byte-for-byte unchanged, and closes ADR-0022 onto the generated SDK. The credential contract (ADR-0048): the token lives only in an httpOnly cookie, the browser speaks only to a same-origin proxy, and the proxy attaches the bearer server-side — so XSS cannot exfiltrate the token and there is no credentialed cross-origin CORS. Changing the proxy path/base, the cookie name/attributes, the session route shape, or the identity contract ripples across the proxy, the session route, the SessionProvider, and the SDK baseUrl. Depends on GET /v1/me + the SDK (E-003) and the AuthContext projection/RBAC (E-018); the sign-in/identity UI is on the token + art system (E-004). OIDC hosted sign-in is a documented seam on the same session route.
